title: Praxeology (Π)
description: How we work - WoW patterns, delegation strategies, best practices

full-stack-story-pattern.what: Include all 4 layers (canonical, data, API, UI) in single story for complete feature delivery
full-stack-story-pattern.when: Implementing new MoneyWorks entities or any user-facing feature
full-stack-story-pattern.how: Break story into phases: Phase 1 (Canonical) → Phase 2 (Data) → Phase 3 (API) → Phase 4 (UI) → Phase 5 (Testing)
full-stack-story-pattern.why: Partial implementations (e.g., US-001 with only canonical + data) leave features incomplete and require follow-up stories
tradeoffs: Longer execution time (~1.5 hours vs ~30 minutes for partial)
evidence: US-002: Account in 'available' status immediately
US-001: Product requires follow-up story for API layer

parallel-task-execution-pattern.what: Execute independent tasks in parallel using multiple Task tool calls in single message
parallel-task-execution-pattern.when: Tasks have no dependencies and can run concurrently
parallel-task-execution-pattern.how: Identify dependency graph, group independent tasks, send single message with multiple Task tool calls
parallel-task-execution-pattern.why: Sequential execution wastes time when tasks could run concurrently
evidence: US-002: T-016/T-017 parallel, T-028/T-029 parallel reduced time from ~2h to ~1.5h

reference-implementation-lookup.what: Use Librarian agent to find existing patterns before implementing new entities
reference-implementation-lookup.when: Starting implementation of new entity following established patterns
reference-implementation-lookup.how: Query Librarian for similar implementations (e.g., 'find TaxRate and Name patterns for Account')
reference-implementation-lookup.why: Leverages proven patterns, ensures consistency, reduces decision-making time
evidence: US-002: Consulted TaxRate and Name patterns for Account implementation

specialized-agent-delegation.what: Delegate tasks to specialized agents (backend-dev, backend-qa, frontend-dev, frontend-qa) via Task tool
specialized-agent-delegation.why: Specialization improves code quality and maintains clear separation of concerns
evidence: US-002: 25 tasks delegated across 4 agent types

context-compaction-recovery.what: Resume work cleanly after context compaction using state files and todo lists
context-compaction-recovery.how: Read state files, check todo status, identify current task, continue from where left off
context-compaction-recovery.why: Context compaction loses in-flight work details, state files preserve progress
evidence: ACCT-001: Resumed T-007 typecheck after compaction, completed successfully

entity-implementation-skill-opportunity.what: After 2-3 entity implementations, create scaffolding skill to automate boilerplate
entity-implementation-skill-opportunity.when: Pattern is proven across multiple entities (Product, Account, Name)
entity-implementation-skill-opportunity.how: Create skill at .claude/skills/moneyworks-entity-scaffolding/ with SKILL.md + scripts
entity-implementation-skill-opportunity.why: Deterministic scripts eliminate LLM inconsistency in file structure, naming, imports
evidence: ACCT-001 retrospective: Identified automation opportunity

evidence-based-validation.what: Collect concrete evidence (file paths, line numbers, test output, API responses) for each acceptance criterion
evidence-based-validation.how: For each AC, gather specific evidence: TypeScript compilation output, test results, API endpoint responses, grep-based verification
evidence-based-validation.why: Objective validation prevents false positives and provides clear audit trail
evidence: US-002: Created validation report with evidence for all 27 ACs

static-validation-fallback.what: Use TypeScript compilation + API testing + grep validation when browser testing blocked
static-validation-fallback.when: Dev server crashes or environment prevents browser testing
static-validation-fallback.how: Verify: (1) TypeScript compiles with zero errors, (2) Routes registered via grep, (3) API endpoints accessible, (4) Navigation updated
static-validation-fallback.why: Environmental issues shouldn't block story completion when implementation is correct
evidence: US-002: 25/27 ACs validated via static analysis despite dev server crashes

table-registry-registration-checklist.what: Mandatory checklist for API layer completion
steps: 5. Verify entity in 'available' list via GET /tables
table-registry-registration-checklist.why: Prevents US-001 mistake where Product was left in 'upcoming' status
evidence: US-002: Account in 'available' after following checklist
US-001: Product in 'upcoming' without registration

automation-readiness-threshold.what: After 3-4 similar entity implementations, create scaffolding skill with deterministic scripts
automation-readiness-threshold.when: Pattern is confirmed across multiple entities (Product, Account, Name, Contact)
automation-readiness-threshold.why: CONT-001 is 4th entity - pattern is clearly repeatable, 9-file structure is 100% deterministic
evidence: CONT-001: 4th entity confirmed pattern - ready for automation

rich-query-methods-practice.what: Add 10-20+ specialized query methods covering common business scenarios
rich-query-methods-practice.how: Beyond CRUD, add domain-specific queries: findByType, findOutstandingInvoices, findByDateRange, findByJob, findByAccount, etc.
rich-query-methods-practice.why: Rich query methods significantly improve DX for common business operations
benefits: Developers don't need to construct complex filters
Common operations are one method call
evidence: TXNS-001: 12 query methods in TransactionRepository, 10 in DetailRepository - 22 total specialized queries

validate-before-import.what: Always validate records client-side before sending to MoneyWorks import
validate-before-import.how: Use validateRecordsForImport() from import-validator.ts before smartImport()
validate-before-import.why: MoneyWorks error responses can be cryptic - client validation provides better messages
benefits: Better error messages with field-level detail
Validates date format (YYYYMMDD), string length, required fields
tradeoffs: Must keep validators in sync with schema
evidence: IMPT-001 L-003: Validation before send saves round trips

use-replace-mode-default.what: Default to 'replace' (upsert) mode for import operations instead of 'insert'
use-replace-mode-default.how: Set mode: 'replace' as default in ImportOptions
use-replace-mode-default.why: Most common use case is update-if-exists, create-if-not - reduces duplicate key errors
benefits: Better developer experience - most imports just work
No need to check existence before import
tradeoffs: May accidentally overwrite data if key collision not expected
evidence: IMPT-001 D-001: Changed default from insert to replace for better DX

mirror-existing-package-structure.what: When creating new packages, mirror the structure of existing similar packages
mirror-existing-package-structure.when: Adding new React Router 7 / Tailwind web packages to monorepo
mirror-existing-package-structure.how: Copy vite.config.ts, react-router.config.ts, tsconfig.json, app/routes.ts patterns from packages/web
mirror-existing-package-structure.why: Ensures consistency, reduces cognitive load, leverages proven configurations
evidence: AIPOC-001: Mirrored packages/web structure for packages/ai - all 12 ACs passed first try

unique-port-per-package.what: Assign unique dev server ports to each web package to avoid conflicts
unique-port-per-package.when: Adding new React Router / Vite packages
unique-port-per-package.how: Increment from existing packages: web=5174, ai=5175, next=5176, etc.
unique-port-per-package.why: Prevents port conflicts when running multiple dev servers simultaneously
evidence: AIPOC-001: Used port 5175 for packages/ai to avoid conflict with packages/web on 5174

tool-error-string-pattern.what: Return error strings from Anthropic SDK tools instead of throwing exceptions
tool-error-string-pattern.when: Implementing tool run functions with betaTool
tool-error-string-pattern.how: Wrap tool logic in try-catch, return descriptive error string on failure
tool-error-string-pattern.why: toolRunner expects tools to return results, not throw - errors returned as strings let Claude see and respond to failures
benefits: Claude receives tool feedback even on errors
Better error context for AI reasoning
tradeoffs: Must remember to catch all exceptions
Error strings less structured than Error objects
evidence: AITOOLS-001 AC-007: Tool errors caught and returned as results

security-module-separation.what: Create dedicated security.ts module for path validation and access control
security-module-separation.when: Implementing file-system tools for AI agents
security-module-separation.how: Single validatePath() function checks: within PROJECT_ROOT, no symlink escapes, resolves relative paths
security-module-separation.why: Centralized security logic ensures consistent validation across all file tools
evidence: AITOOLS-001 AC-004: security.ts validates all paths against PROJECT_ROOT

loom-full-cycle-delegation.what: Use /loom:run for complete feature delivery: ideate → plan → execute → finalize
loom-full-cycle-delegation.when: User requests a new feature with clear requirements
loom-full-cycle-delegation.how: Single command delegates to loom-ideator, loom-planner, loom-executor, loom-finalizer sequentially
loom-full-cycle-delegation.why: Full cycle ensures story is properly tracked, planned, executed, and learnings captured
benefits: Complete feature delivery in one command
evidence: AIPOC-001 + AITOOLS-001: Both delivered via /loom:run full cycle

incremental-artifact-component-implement.what: Build UI artifact components one type at a time with consistent patterns
incremental-artifact-component-implement.when: Implementing multiple related UI components (charts, tables, metrics)
incremental-artifact-component-implement.how: Create first component as reference, then replicate pattern for each additional type
incremental-artifact-component-implement.why: Ensures consistent structure, imports, and styling across all artifact renderers
benefits: Easy to add new artifact types
tradeoffs: Initial time investment in establishing pattern
Must resist urge to prematurely abstract
evidence: ARTF-001: 9 artifact components implemented with consistent pattern

artifact-scaffolding-automation-opportun.what: After implementing 9+ artifact types, create scaffolding skill for future artifact additions
artifact-scaffolding-automation-opportun.when: Pattern is proven across multiple artifact implementations
artifact-scaffolding-automation-opportun.how: Create skill that generates: type interface, component file, renderer case, system prompt update
artifact-scaffolding-automation-opportun.why: Deterministic scaffolding eliminates manual boilerplate and ensures consistency
benefits: 15-20 minutes saved per new artifact type
No forgotten renderer cases or type exports
evidence: ARTF-001: 9 artifact types revealed repeatable 4-step pattern for new types

typescript-conditional-spread-ternary.what: Use ternary operators for conditional spread in TypeScript objects
typescript-conditional-spread-ternary.when: Adding optional properties to objects based on conditions
typescript-conditional-spread-ternary.why: TypeScript && operator returns boolean false when condition is falsy, causing TS2698 error
evidence: DTXN-001 L-001: Discovered during daily transaction summary implementation

defensive-artifact-rendering.what: Use defensive coding with fallbacks when rendering AI-generated artifacts
defensive-artifact-rendering.when: Building artifact React components that receive AI-generated data
defensive-artifact-rendering.how: Provide default values for all optional fields: const suppliers = data.suppliers || []; const currency = data.currency || 'NZD';
defensive-artifact-rendering.why: AI-generated data structures may vary or have missing fields - defensive coding provides better UX than rendering nothing
benefits: Component renders even with partial data
evidence: APAY-001 D-001: Chose defensive pattern from department-pnl.tsx over strict type guards

moneyworks-canonical-terminology-in-ui.what: Preserve exact MoneyWorks terminology in UI labels (e.g., 'as at' not 'as of')
moneyworks-canonical-terminology-in-ui.when: Building UI components that display MoneyWorks report data
moneyworks-canonical-terminology-in-ui.how: Match exact field labels from MoneyWorks reports: 'Accounts Payable as at', 'GST' not 'Tax', aging bucket names
moneyworks-canonical-terminology-in-ui.why: Maintains canonical DSL purity and matches what users expect from MoneyWorks reports
tradeoffs: May seem non-standard to non-MW users
evidence: APAY-001 L-002: Preserved 'as at' terminology from MoneyWorks
